title: Oxyceros penangianus
taxonomy: Oxyceros penangianus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in the regions of Peninsular Malaysia and southern Thailand. This species is further classified within the genus Oxyceros, which is part of the Rubiaceae family. Oxyceros penangianus is closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Oxyceros scaphium, sharing similar characteristics and habitats. The classification of Oxyceros penangianus is essential for understanding its evolutionary history and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Oxyceros penangianus, also known as the Penang Hill Balsam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxyceros penangianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusions, and is also used to make herbal teas and tonics.
